title: Only 0.02pct of crimes involve Rohingya, says IGP
description:
Only 0.02 per cent of crimes committed in Malaysia involve Rohingya individuals, according to Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail. He said crime should not be generalised to an entire community and stressed that enforcement actions are taken against individuals who break the law, regardless of nationality. 

The IGP also reiterated that the Rohingya issue requires a comprehensive and coordinated approach involving multiple agencies and regional cooperation, given its humanitarian, security and immigration dimensions.

Read more at:

title: Urban poor not sidelined, Bukit Kiara project proves rights upheld
description:
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Bukit Kiara development demonstrates the government's commitment to ensuring that the urban poor are not sidelined in the country's development agenda. 

He stressed that development must be inclusive and benefit all segments of society, particularly lower-income groups. 

Anwar said the project reflects the government's efforts to balance economic growth, housing needs and social equity, while ensuring that opportunities and basic amenities remain accessible to those in need.

title: Trailer driver in fatal Karak Highway crash tests positive for drugs
description:
The trailer driver involved in a fatal crash that killed four people at Km43 Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway yesterday has tested positive for drugs.

District police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said urine screening confirmed that the suspect had consumed methamphetamine.

He said the driver was brought to the Bentong police headquarters after undergoing treatment at Bentong Hospital.

Yesterday, Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said checks revealed that the lorry driver had 15 traffic summonses.

The 4.25pm crash yesterday claimed the lives of four people, including an infant, after the car they were travelling in was crushed by the overturned trailer.

The victims — a man, two women and a year-old child — were killed on the spot, while the lorry driver sustained head and hip injuries.



Read more at:

description:

title: Final family photo becomes last memory after Karak Expressway tragedy
description:
A family photograph taken shortly before a fatal crash has become a heartbreaking final memory for the loved ones of four victims killed in the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway tragedy. The victims, including an infant, died after their car was crushed by an overturned trailer at Km43 of the highway. 

The tragedy has sparked an outpouring of grief on social media, with the family's final photo serving as a poignant reminder of how quickly lives can change. Authorities are continuing investigations into the crash.

title: Good ties with major powers do not compromise national principles – Anwar
description:
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia’s strong ties with major powers such as China and the United States have never compromised the country’s principles or national interests.

Speaking at the Pakatan Harapan rally "Keadilan Untuk Bangsa Johor", he said Malaysia remains friends with all nations while maintaining firm positions on issues such as Gaza, trade, investment and law enforcement.

Anwar added that good relations with countries including China, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, Egypt and the US have strengthened Malaysia’s international standing and created greater opportunities for the country.

title: Highland Towers: Final chapter as demolition looms
description:
The long-awaited demolition of the remaining Highland Towers blocks appears closer than ever, marking what could be the final chapter of one of Malaysia’s most tragic building disasters. More than three decades after the 1993 collapse that claimed 48 lives, legal and administrative hurdles are finally being resolved to pave the way for demolition. 

The abandoned structures in Hulu Kelang have stood as a stark reminder of the tragedy for over 30 years. Authorities say the remaining blocks are expected to be demolished by the end of the year, bringing closure to a site that has long symbolised the importance of building safety and accountability.

Malaysia's long-standing programme to resettle Rohingya refugees and other displaced persons to third countries has slowed significantly and is now largely at a standstill due to challenges faced by receiving nations, says Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail.

title: MARKET PULSE AM JUNE 15, 2026
description:
Pentech made a strong debut on the ACE Market, surging 44 per cent to open at 29 sen, compared with its IPO price of 20 sen.

About 90 million shares changed hands during early trading, giving it a market capitalisation of RM161.2 million.

Bursa Malaysia opened on a firmer footing as investor sentiment improved following reports of a peace agreement between the United States and Iran, as well as the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

The benchmark index opened at around 1,690 and is expected to trade within the 1,680 to 1,690 range throughout the day.

In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin is trading at RM264,549, while Ethereum hovered at RM6,931.

That’s it for Market Pulse.

title: Loan sharks getting more aggressive and deceptive, warns Michael Chong
description:
Malaysia's loan shark problem is escalating, with MCA receiving 159 complaints involving nearly RM16 million in loans as of June. 

Its Public Services and Complaints Department head Datuk Seri Michael Chong warned that syndicates are using increasingly deceptive tactics, including charging victims for red-paint attacks they orchestrated themselves, and imposing interest as high as RM21,000 on a RM1,000 loan.

title: Police investigating viral claims linking Rosmah to US$13mil property purchase
description:
Police are investigating viral social media claims linking Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor to the alleged purchase of a US$13 million luxury property for her son-in-law. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said preliminary findings suggest the accounts circulating the allegations may be fake. 

Authorities are working with relevant agencies, including the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), to verify the authenticity of the claims. Police have urged the public not to speculate while investigations are ongoing.

title: Anwar: People suffer when politicians stir racial tensions
description:
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned that ordinary Malaysians are the ones who suffer when politicians exploit racial issues for political gain. He urged political leaders to focus on justice, good governance and national development instead of stoking racial tensions for support. 

Anwar stressed that Malaysia's strength lies in its diversity and unity, calling on all parties to reject divisive rhetoric that could undermine social harmony and national progress.

title: MTSB bill to be tabled in October Parliament sitting, says Loke
description:
The Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB) is progressing as planned, but the bill to formally establish it will not be tabled in the upcoming Parliament session.

Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the MTSB has received Cabinet approval for its establishment, but discussions are still ongoing on its responsibilities and legal framework.

title: KL's 10,000 CCTV cameras an effective tool to support law enforcement, efficiency, says IGP
description:
The installation of 10,000 closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras across Kuala Lumpur since 2020 has significantly enhanced police investigations and operational efficiency, says Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail. 

The Inspector-General of Police said the extensive CCTV network has proven to be an effective tool in supporting law enforcement efforts, particularly as the police force continues to leverage technology to improve public safety.

title: "Don't bet what you can't afford”: World Cup betting fuels loan shark trap
description:
Loan shark and online gambling syndicates are believed to be operated by the same criminal groups and the World Cup season is making things worse. 

MCA Youth deputy chief Mike Chong Yew Chuan warned that gamblers who lose money during major sporting events are frequently targeted by loan sharks offering quick loans to cover their debts.
